item.title: Turkish Inflation Quickens More Than Expected to 32.4% in April
item.description: Turkish annual inflation rose more than expected in April as higher prices driven by the Iran war continued to reverberate across the economy.

item.title: UAE Oil Head Says OPEC Exit Gives Ability to Speed Up Investment
item.description: The United Arab Emirates’ surprise exit from OPEC gives it greater ability to accelerate investment and expand, the head of the country’s state-run oil company said.

item.title: European Gas Drops as Tanker Attack Clouds US Plan for Hormuz
item.description: European natural gas prices edged lower, as reports of an attack on a tanker raised doubts about a US plan to begin guiding some neutral ships trapped in the Persian Gulf out through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Asian Buyers Offer Big Premium for Diesel-Rich Crude From UAE
item.description: Some Asian refiners offered to buy Upper Zakum crude from the United Arab Emirates at around $20 a barrel above official prices, as processors scramble for medium-sour grades after the Iran war disrupted supply.

item.title: Samsung Shares Lag SK Hynix’s Rally as Analysts Cite Strike Risk
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. shares’ recent underperformance against SK Hynix Inc. may intensify as a looming workers’ strike weighs on South Korea’s most valuable company versus its rival chipmaker.

item.title: What Is Hantavirus and How Did It Kill Cruise Ship Passengers?
item.description: A cluster of hantavirus infections linked to a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean has left three people dead and several others seriously ill, prompting an alert from the World Health Organization and raising questions about how a rare rodent-borne disease might spread in such an unusual setting.

item.title: Montenegro Says EU Working to Fix Euro Issue Hindering Accession
item.description: The European Union is working on resolving the thorny issue of Montenegro’s unilateral use of the euro, an obstacle for the Adriatic nation’s plans to join the bloc, Finance Minister Novica Vukovic said.

item.title: Quant Fund Sees Hungary Finance Revival as Orban Era Is Ending
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s defeat at the ballot box unleashed a wave of enthusiasm over potential opportunities in Hungary’s financial sector. A small quant shop with plans to expand in Budapest is adding to the chorus of voices expecting a revival.

item.title: Singapore Air to Add Faster Starlink Inflight Wi-Fi From 2027
item.description: Singapore Airlines Ltd. said it will begin rolling out a Starlink broadband service on its flights from 2027, with the system promising faster connectivity than its existing inflight offering.

item.title: Yen Jumps 0.8% in Asia Trading, Putting Investors on High Alert
item.description: The yen jumped in Asia on Monday, as traders remained on edge over the potential for Japanese authorities to step back into the market after last week’s intervention to curb declines.

item.title: Singapore and New Zealand Sign Essential Supply Trade Pact
item.description: Singapore and New Zealand signed a deal Monday to strengthen supply chain resilience and safeguard the flow of critical goods such as food and energy, as both countries seek to shield their trade-dependent economies against disruptions from the war in Iran.

item.title: Swiss Lawmakers Gather to Discuss Law That Will Shape UBS Future
item.description: A group of 13 Swiss lawmakers will meet in Bern on Monday to kickstart the legislative process that will shape the future of UBS Group AG.

item.title: Japan Can Conduct 30 More Yen Interventions, Goldman Sachs Says
item.description: Japan has the firepower to intervene 30 times in currency markets at last week’s scale, according to analysts at Goldman Sachs Group Inc., though officials are expected to conserve its reserves and step in at more effective moments.

item.title: Why Markets Think the US Is Winning the Blockade
item.description: Europe, on the other hand...

item.title: ASX Warns Firms About ‘Ramping’ AI Upside to Push Stock Prices
item.description: Australia’s stock exchange operator warned businesses not to exaggerate the impact of artificial intelligence on their operations, saying it monitors the market for instances of so-called ‘ramping’ up of share prices.

item.title: Hong Kong Property Shares Rally as Morgan Stanley Feeds Optimism
item.description: Hong Kong property shares rallied, leading gains in the broader market along with technology stocks, after Morgan Stanley boosted its forecast for home prices.

item.title: History Points to More Upside for India’s Small Caps After April’s 18% Surge
item.description: Iran war risks and earnings are set to drive the broader market in May.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Rise to Record High on Tech Boost
item.description: Emerging-market assets gained, with strong tech earnings and hopes of a resumption in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz lifting sentiment.

item.title: AI Chipmakers in Korea, Taiwan Drive Asian Stocks to Near Record
item.description: Asian stocks rose to near a record, with a measure excluding Japan already at an all-time high, as investors’ enthusiasm for artificial intelligence bets helped erase losses caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Top Bank of Korea Official Says It’s Time to Consider Rate Hike
item.description: A senior official at the Bank of Korea said it’s time to consider raising rates, as economic growth appears unlikely to fall much below the bank’s earlier projections, while inflation is likely to exceed its previous forecast.

item.title: Why Korean Pension Fund’s New Hedging Policy Supports Won
item.description: South Korea’s largest pension fund removed its cap on currency hedging last month, allowing it to exercise more heft in the foreign exchange market at a time of won weakness.

item.title: China’s Unprecedented Defiance of US Sanctions Triggers Showdown
item.description: China has ordered its companies to ignore US sanctions, an unprecedented act of defiance that threatens to trap a vast banking sector in the crossfire as tension rises between the world’s largest economies.

item.title: Modi Eyes Milestone Win as Key Indian States Begin Vote Counts
item.description: India begins counting votes in several key states on Monday, providing a barometer of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s popularity in the face of heightened economic challenges.

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Leaves 3 Dead, WHO Says
item.description: Three people have died and one is in intensive care in South Africa after a cluster of hantavirus infections linked to a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, the World Health Organization said Sunday.

item.title: Meme Stock GameStop Pitches $56 Billion Takeover of eBay
item.description: GameStop Corp. is proposing to buy eBay Inc. for about $56 billion in cash and stock, a bold attempt by Ryan Cohen to take over a storied e-commerce name several times larger.

item.title: Block Trade King Simon Sadler Begins High-Profile Court Showdown
item.description: The crowd at Bloomfield Road Stadium chanted Simon Sadler’s name, giving the financier a hero’s welcome.

item.title: Asia Bond Sales Jump in April as War Lull Spurs Rush in Issuance
item.description: Asia-Pacific’s dollar bond market saw its strongest April in five years as the start of a ceasefire in the Iran war opened the floodgates to a rush of issuance.

item.title: Gold Steady With Focus on Hormuz Reopening, Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Gold was little changed as traders monitored President Donald Trump’s plan to start guiding some ships through the Strait of Hormuz and progress toward a US-Iran deal.

item.title: Aussie Rally May Fade as RBA Set to Temper Hikes, Analysts Say
item.description: The Australian dollar’s rally may fade with the Reserve Bank of Australia set to signal a slower pace of interest rate hikes after a widely expected increase this week, according to top forecasters.

item.title: US Futures Gain, Oil Falls on Signs of Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
item.description: US stock futures rose and oil fell as improving risk sentiment was buoyed by prospects of shipping resuming through the Strait of Hormuz and progress toward a US-Iran deal.
